rulings.Case Title: VALERIE ANN CARLING et al VS. ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosection Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procesure Section 583.420(A)(2)(A)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss action for delay in prosecution Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 583.420(A)(2)(A) is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless counsel for the moving party informs plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and plaintiffs say that they do not wish to contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLYDE DYKEMAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Bill Whitley Painting & Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos or an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant due to its failure to refer to contention interrogatory responses in its separate statement. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TOCCHINI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration regarding Cranite creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s D¿Amico and other objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEE NIXT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant California Institute of Technology¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production as to either its employees¿ conduct or asbestos content due to its failure to provide contention interrogatory responses on either issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK RICCO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.S Motion To Show Cause Re Dismissal On Grounds Of Forum Non-Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'s Joinder To Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.'s Motion Re Dismissal. On the Court¿s order to show cause why this case should not be dismissed, Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion seeking dismissal on Grounds of Forum Non-Conveniens and all joinders thereto, the case is dismissed. While plaintiff may have the unqualified right to dismiss his federal court case, his decision to file both the federal court case and this lawsuit shows that he is unconcerned about two parallel lawsuits proceeding at the same time. Since Judge Alvarado has already determined that this court is a seriously inconvenient forum and the four defendants that appear not to be amenable to jurisdiction in Nevada can be sued in a federal lawsuit venued in the Northern District of California, there are alternate suitable fora for all defendants named in this case. Plaintiff¿s concerns about the manner in which the federal court system has handled asbestos cases in the past, even if true and pertinent to the present, are not a basis to retain this lawsuit in San Francisco Superior Court. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVEA MERLE REYNOLDS VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVEA MERLE REYNOLDS VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Foster Wheeler, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted. Liberally construed, the declaration of Mr. Lineaweaver and the deposition of Mr. McDown at 16:26-18:3 create a triable issue as to Mr. Williamson¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s withdrawal of her punitive damages claim requires granting of the alternate motion for summary adjudication. =(220/HEK)
